text.title: xUnit Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the xUnit + Moq + WAF testing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every reachable branch in production code MUST be covered by at least one `[Fact]` or `[Theory]` `InlineData` row. Tests MUST name the branch via `MethodUnderTest_State_ExpectedBehaviour`.

rationale.source: Roy Osherove — Art of Unit Testing

rule.rationale: Generic test names ("UserServiceTest1") hide what regression a failure represents; the convention turns CI output into a self-documenting bug report.

rule.statement: Test fixtures MUST inject dependencies via the constructor — never via `[OneTimeSetUp]`/`[TestInitialize]` equivalents. State that survives across tests MUST live in an `IClassFixture<T>`.
rule.rationale: xUnit creates a new instance per test; constructor injection makes the lifecycle explicit and prevents hidden cross-test state.

rule.statement: Assertions on collections, complex objects, or extracted fields MUST use FluentAssertions (`actual.Should().BeEquivalentTo(expected)`). Raw `Assert.Equal(list1, list2)` is forbidden for non-trivial structures.
rule.rationale: FluentAssertions' diff is readable; `Assert.Equal` on collections emits unreadable `[1, 2, 3]` differs from `[1, 2, 4]` with no clue which element failed.

rule.statement: Integration tests MUST use `WebApplicationFactory<Program>` (or equivalent), replacing real external dependencies via `builder.ConfigureTestServices(...)`. `HttpClient` MUST be constructed from `factory.CreateClient()` — never `new HttpClient()`.
rule.rationale: The factory boots the actual DI graph + middleware; ad-hoc `new HttpClient` bypasses the host and tests nothing.

rule.statement: Data-layer tests MUST run against the real database engine via Testcontainers (e.g. `PostgreSqlContainer`). `UseInMemoryDatabase` is forbidden for tests that exercise queries, constraints, or migrations.
rule.rationale: InMemory diverges from real DBs on JSONB ops, locking, FK behaviour; tests pass in CI and fail in prod.

rule.statement: CI MUST enforce a coverage gate: line ≥ 70 %, branch ≥ 60 %, via Coverlet + ReportGenerator. Build fails when thresholds drop.
rule.rationale: Coverage without a gate is advisory only; agents skip uncovered paths unless the build blocks.
